selected_important_turn.text: I think that I should get 2 firewood, 1 food and 2 water
selected_important_turn.why_important: This was the agent's first concrete counter-offer after the opponent explicitly asked what the agent considered fair. It set a competitive, zero-sum tone and locked the agent into claiming a large share of desirable items, closing off integrative possibilities when the opponent had signaled openness.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Responding with a hard self-favoring offer immediately after a prosocial prompt missed a clear opportunity to explore tradeoffs (e.g., ask which items the opponent values most or propose multiple packages). The move needlessly escalated competition and reduced chances to increase joint value or reach a more balanced split.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of issuing a firm self-centered allocation, shift to a collaborative proposal: ask a question to elicit the opponent's priorities or offer two or three package options that trade off items (e.g., I can take the firewood if you get more food), or explicitly invite a deal that balances both parties' top needs.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — exploring trades or multi-option offers creates opportunities to enlarge the pie.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — trade-based proposals make concessions explicit and can produce more balanced outcomes.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — presenting options and asking preferences lowers friction and speeds agreement.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — a collaborative framing reduces adversarial tone and builds trust.

value2reason.Low: I brought a filtration kit so I can make my own drinking water from the nearby creek.
value2reason.Medium: Some of the food I brought has spoiled so I need extra food.
value2reason.High: My sleeping bag is thin and not very warm so I need extra firewood to keep myself warm at night.

value2reason.Low: I would require water over food, as it is a more essential than food.
value2reason.Medium: I am a good cook, and therefore allowing me to have the food, I would ensure that meals are at the very minimum decent.
value2reason.High: I have a medical condition that requires me to not get cold. The firewood is a life or death essential for me.

dialogue.text: Hello! Which item do you need the most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I would love to have the Firewood the most.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Unfortunately I need firewood the most too. How about I take 2 firewood, 2 food, and 1 water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I feel that I am not getting a fair deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Then what do you think is fair?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that I should get 2 firewood, 1 food and 2 water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your concern. Let's compromise: I'll take 2 firewood, 1 food, and 1 water. You can have 1 firewood, 2 food, and 2 water. It's fair and covers all packages.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I value firewood most for my medical needs. How about if I take 3 firewood and you get 2 food and 1 water? It balances our priorities while ensuring I have what I need most.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need firewood the most for warmth. If you take 3 firewood, I’ll take 2 food and 1 water. It’s fair and meets both our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need firewood most for my medical condition. Let’s find a balance—how about I take 2 firewood, 1 food, and 1 water, and you get 1 firewood, 2 food, and 2 water? It respects my priority while ensuring you have enough.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
